memes.label: "IV. POSITION OF THE COMMUNISTS IN RELATION TO THE VARIOUS EXISTING OPPOSITION PARTIES"
Class Antagonism
memes.description: The fundamental opposition between exploiting and exploited classes that drives historical development — in the modern epoch, simplified to the antagonism between bourgeoisie and proletariat.

memes.label: "Marx's Theory of History in the Communist Manifesto"
Mode of Production
memes.description: The historically specific way in which a society organises material production — the ensemble of productive forces and relations of production — which constitutes the economic foundation on which the legal, political, and ideological superstructure rises.

memes.label: Productive Forces
memes.description: The material powers of production — technology, labour-power, science, and natural resources — whose development drives historical change and which, under capitalism, have grown so colossal that they periodically revolt against the bourgeois property relations that fetter them.

memes.label: Relations of Production
memes.description: The social relations — above all, property relations — into which human beings necessarily enter in the process of material production, which correspond to a definite stage of the productive forces and form the economic structure of society.

memes.label: Utopian Socialism
memes.description: The pre-Marxian socialist doctrines of Saint-Simon, Fourier, Owen, and their followers, which critiqued existing society and envisioned ideal communities but rejected class struggle as the means of transformation — analysed sympathetically yet critically in Ch. III §3 of the Manifesto.
